page: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It is equipped with 3 shared Intel Core Broadwell vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, featuring 3 physical cores with a single thread per core. The memory configuration provides 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per core. Graphics processing is handled by a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU, which delivers 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 170 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a strong specialization in GPU-accelerated tasks, contrasted by modest CPU performance. In LLM inference tests, the server achieves top-tier results, processing 8612.59 tokens/second for prompts on a small 135M model and 600.63 tokens/second on a medium 7B model. Text generation speeds are also strong, reaching 286.04 tokens/second and 27.65 tokens/second for the small and medium models, respectively. Conversely, CPU-bound benchmarks are weak, with a Passmark CPU score of 5490.08 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 5146.49.

The resource profile of this server offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for specialized workloads. By utilizing a shared CPU allocation and a fractional GPU, it lowers the entry barrier for projects requiring hardware acceleration without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The primary tradeoff is the older Broadwell CPU architecture and shared CPU model, which limit raw computational throughput and multi-threaded processing efficiency outside of GPU-accelerated tasks. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks at 41.0 GB/sec but weak for small cached blocks.

This server is best suited for small to medium LLM inference, machine learning model serving, and GPU-accelerated data processing. It is also appropriate for lightweight graphics rendering and parallel workloads that fit within the 8 GB VRAM limit. It is not recommended for CPU-intensive multi-threaded computing, high-throughput web serving, or database operations requiring high sequential CPU performance.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-3c-32g-8vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring 3 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. It includes 170 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference, with prompt processing speeds of 600.63 tokens/second on a 7B model. However, CPU-bound performance is weak, as indicated by a Passmark CPU score of 5490.08 and a weak multi-core stress-ng result of 5146.49. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU workloads by trading off raw CPU compute power for high memory density and dedicated GPU acceleration. It is best suited for small to medium LLM inference, machine learning model serving, and GPU-accelerated data processing tasks.

tagline: A fractional GPU server combining high memory capacity and NVMe storage for cost-efficient machine learning inference and data processing.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 8 GB VRAM
3 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs with 32.0 GB of RAM
170 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds
Suited for small-to-medium model inference and GPU-accelerated tasks
